Title: Direct object reference on patient record by sequential internal patient id
Preconditions: Two patients P1 and P2 exist; a low-privileged user authenticated who is authorized only for P1
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as the limited user and load P1 via the patient dashboard | 2. Capture the patient REST/UI request containing P1 internal id | 3. Replay the request substituting P2 internal id | 4. Observe the response
Expected_Result: Server returns 403/404 with no P2 PHI; access denial is recorded to the audit log; no demographics leak in error body

Title: IDOR on clinical observation (obs) resource across patients
Preconditions: User authorized for P1 only; an obs belonging to P2 exists with a known uuid
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as the limited user | 2. Issue GET /ws/rest/v1/obs/{P2_obs_uuid} | 3. Inspect response status and body | 4. Repeat via FHIR Observation/{id}
Expected_Result: Both REST and FHIR endpoints deny access (403/404); no observation value, code or concept returned; denial audited

Title: Horizontal escalation on encounter resource via REST
Preconditions: Two clinicians each own distinct encounters; clinician A authenticated
Test_Steps: 1. As clinician A list own encounters | 2. Note an encounter uuid owned by clinician B for an unauthorized patient | 3. GET /ws/rest/v1/encounter/{B_uuid} | 4. Attempt DELETE on the same uuid
Expected_Result: Read and delete are both denied; no encounter detail disclosed; privilege check enforced server-side not just hidden in UI

Title: Privilege escalation by calling admin-only manageUsers endpoint as clinician
Preconditions: A clinician account lacking Manage Users privilege is authenticated
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as clinician | 2. Attempt POST /ws/rest/v1/user to create a new admin user | 3. Attempt PUT to grant System Developer role to self | 4. Observe responses
Expected_Result: All privilege-mutating calls return 403; no user created; no role granted; attempt logged

Sub_Module: A01 Broken Access Control - Force Browse
Title: Forced browsing to admin pages without menu link
Preconditions: Non-admin user authenticated
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as non-admin | 2. Navigate directly to /admin and to module management URLs by typing the path | 3. Observe rendering and data exposure
Expected_Result: Server enforces authorization and redirects/denies; no admin functions or settings shown despite direct URL entry

Sub_Module: A01 Broken Access Control - FHIR scope
Title: Patient-context FHIR token cannot read other patients (compartment)
Preconditions: SMART patient-launch token issued for patient/Patient.read scoped to P1
Test_Steps: 1. Present the patient-scoped token | 2. GET /fhir2/R4/Patient/{P1} | 3. GET /fhir2/R4/Patient/{P2} | 4. GET /fhir2/R4/Observation?patient={P2}
Expected_Result: P1 reads succeed; any cross-compartment read to P2 is denied with 403/empty bundle; compartment enforced server-side

Sub_Module: A01 Broken Access Control - Decision Table
Title: Resource access decision table across role x operation
Preconditions: Roles {Anonymous, Clinician, Pharmacist, Admin} and operations {read, write, delete} on Patient/Order
Test_Steps: 1. For each role authenticate | 2. Attempt each operation on Patient and Order | 3. Record allow/deny | 4. Compare against expected matrix
Test_Data: Matrix: Anonymous=deny all; Clinician=read+write Patient/Order, no delete; Pharmacist=read Patient, write Order(dispense); Admin=all
Expected_Result: Every cell matches the authorization matrix; no allow where deny expected; deviations flagged as defects

Sub_Module: A01 Broken Access Control - Mass assignment
Title: Mass assignment of restricted fields on patient update
Preconditions: Clinician authorized to edit demographics for P1
Test_Steps: 1. Capture legitimate PUT to patient | 2. Inject extra fields such as roles, voided, dateCreated, uuid override | 3. Submit | 4. Read back the record
Expected_Result: Server ignores/strips unauthorized fields; patient not voided; no role granted; only permitted demographic fields change

Sub_Module: A01 Broken Access Control - Provider link
Title: Patient cannot access another patient via portal patient/Patient relationship
Preconditions: Patient-portal style account (omiiCARE) bound to P1 only
Test_Steps: 1. Login as patient-portal user | 2. Request own records | 3. Tamper patientId param to P2 | 4. Observe
Expected_Result: Only P1 data returned; P2 request denied; binding between account and patient enforced server-side

Title: TLS configuration rejects weak protocols and ciphers
Preconditions: Access to the owned test environment endpoint and a TLS scanner
Test_Steps: 1. Scan endpoint TLS versions | 2. Attempt TLS 1.0/1.1 handshake | 3. Enumerate cipher suites | 4. Check for export/NULL/RC4/3DES
Expected_Result: Only TLS 1.2+ negotiated; legacy protocols refused; no weak/export ciphers; forward secrecy ciphers preferred

Title: HSTS enforced and HTTP redirects to HTTPS
Preconditions: Owned test environment reachable on port 80 and 443
Test_Steps: 1. Request http:// base URL | 2. Inspect redirect | 3. Inspect Strict-Transport-Security header on https response | 4. Verify max-age and includeSubDomains
Expected_Result: HTTP 301/308 to HTTPS; HSTS present with long max-age and includeSubDomains; no PHI served over cleartext

Title: PHI at rest is encrypted (DB/file/backup design verification)
Preconditions: Design/config access to DB encryption settings on owned environment
Test_Steps: 1. Review DB at-rest encryption (TDE/volume) config | 2. Inspect attachment/blob storage encryption | 3. Inspect backup encryption | 4. Confirm key management separation
Test_Data: Tables: patient, person_name, obs; attachment store; nightly backup
Expected_Result: PHI columns/files and backups are encrypted at rest; keys managed separately from data; documented per policy

Title: Password storage uses strong adaptive hashing with per-user salt
Preconditions: Design/config access to authentication storage on owned environment
Test_Steps: 1. Review password hash algorithm and parameters | 2. Verify unique salt per user | 3. Confirm no reversible/MD5/SHA1-unsalted | 4. Verify hash not exposed via any API
Test_Data: Sample users: u1, u2 with identical passwords
Expected_Result: Hashes use a strong adaptive algorithm with per-user salt; identical passwords yield different hashes; never returned in any response

Title: No sensitive data in URL query strings or referers
Preconditions: Authenticated session with PHI views
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate clinical pages | 2. Inspect URLs and Referer headers | 3. Check for tokens, MRNs, SSNs in query strings | 4. Check server access logs design
Expected_Result: No tokens or PHI in query strings; sensitive identifiers passed in body/headers; referer leakage controlled via Referrer-Policy

Title: SQL injection on patient search name parameter
Preconditions: Authenticated user with patient search access
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient search | 2. Enter SQLi payloads in name field | 3. Submit and observe | 4. Try boolean and time-based variants
Test_Data: Payloads: '' OR ''1''=''1; '' UNION SELECT null--; ''; WAITFOR DELAY ''0:0:5''--
Expected_Result: Input treated as literal; no extra rows, no DB error leak, no time delay; parameterized queries confirmed; attempt logged

Title: Blind SQLi on REST query params (q, identifier)
Preconditions: Authenticated user; REST patient search endpoint reachable
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/rest/v1/patient?q=<payload> | 2. Use time-based payload | 3. Use boolean-based payload | 4. Compare response timing and counts
Expected_Result: No measurable injected delay; identical result counts regardless of boolean condition; queries parameterized

Title: ORM/HQL injection via order-by or filter parameters
Preconditions: Authenticated user with list/sort endpoints
Test_Steps: 1. Identify sortable list endpoint | 2. Inject HQL fragments into sort/filter param | 3. Observe errors or data changes | 4. Try nested property traversal
Expected_Result: Injection rejected; no HQL error stack; cannot traverse to restricted properties; allowlist of sortable fields enforced

Title: LDAP injection on directory-backed login
Preconditions: Environment configured with LDAP/AD authentication backend (owned)
Test_Steps: 1. At login enter LDAP metacharacters in username | 2. Try filter-breaking payloads | 3. Observe auth result and logs
Expected_Result: Login fails safely; no auth bypass; LDAP filter properly escaped; no directory enumeration; attempts logged

Title: OS command injection on report/export filename or path params
Preconditions: User with report export capability
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger export with crafted filename | 2. Inject shell metacharacters | 3. Observe server behavior | 4. Check for command execution side effects
Expected_Result: No command executed; metacharacters sanitized; no file outside allowed dir; safe error returned

Title: NoSQL/JSON injection on FHIR search parameters
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint reachable with search params
Test_Steps: 1. Send FHIR search with operator-injection in params | 2. Use JSON object where scalar expected | 3. Observe parsing | 4. Confirm no filter bypass
Expected_Result: Server validates types; object-injection rejected with 400; no broadened result set; no backend error leak

Title: Stored XSS via patient name / address fields rendered on dashboard
Preconditions: Clinician can register/edit a patient; viewer opens dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Register patient with script payload in given/family name | 2. Save | 3. Open patient dashboard as another user | 4. Observe execution
Expected_Result: Payload stored as data and rendered inert (encoded); no script execution; output encoding applied on all PHI fields

Title: Reflected XSS via search query echoed in results header
Preconditions: Authenticated user with search
Test_Steps: 1. Submit search term containing markup | 2. Observe results page echo of term | 3. Inspect DOM and CSP behavior
Expected_Result: Search term encoded in output; no script runs; CSP blocks inline as defense in depth

Title: HTTP header / CRLF injection on parameters reflected into headers
Preconditions: Endpoint that sets a header/cookie from user input
Test_Steps: 1. Send CRLF payload in input reflected to response header | 2. Observe response splitting | 3. Attempt to set rogue Set-Cookie
Expected_Result: CRLF stripped/encoded; no header injection; no response splitting; single intended header only

Title: Negative-by-default authorization design for new clinical resource
Preconditions: Design review of a newly added resource endpoint
Test_Steps: 1. Review default access policy for the new endpoint | 2. Verify it denies unless explicitly granted | 3. Test an unmapped role | 4. Confirm fail-closed
Expected_Result: Endpoint denies by default; only explicitly privileged roles allowed; fail-closed on missing policy

Title: Account takeover via unbounded password reset flow
Preconditions: Password reset feature enabled on owned environment
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger reset for a victim account | 2. Attempt to brute-force the reset token/OTP | 3. Reuse a token | 4. Use an expired token
Expected_Result: Tokens are high-entropy, single-use, short-lived, rate-limited and bound to the requesting session; brute force blocked

Title: Prevent ordering high-risk medication without required co-sign workflow
Preconditions: Prescriber and required co-signer roles configured for controlled substances
Test_Steps: 1. Place a controlled-substance order as prescriber | 2. Attempt to activate without co-sign | 3. Provide co-sign | 4. Verify state
Expected_Result: Order cannot reach Active without co-sign; bypass attempts blocked; safety control cannot be skipped via API

Title: Username/account enumeration via differential responses
Preconditions: Login and reset endpoints reachable
Test_Steps: 1. Submit login for known vs unknown username | 2. Submit reset for known vs unknown email | 3. Compare status, body, timing
Expected_Result: Responses are uniform for known/unknown (same message, status, timing); no account enumeration possible

Title: Security response headers present and correct
Preconditions: Authenticated and anonymous access to owned environment
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect response headers on app pages and API | 2. Verify CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options/frame-ancestors, Referrer-Policy | 3. Verify no Server/X-Powered-By version leak
Test_Data: Expected: X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff; CSP set; frame-ancestors restricted
Expected_Result: All baseline security headers present and restrictive; no sensitive version/banner disclosure

Title: Default and demo credentials are disabled in non-demo deployments
Preconditions: Hardened deployment profile (not the public demo)
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt login with known default admin/Admin123 | 2. Attempt common module default accounts | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Default credentials rejected in hardened profile; forced password change on first admin login; documented

Title: Error pages do not leak stack traces or internals
Preconditions: Ability to trigger 4xx/5xx on owned environment
Test_Steps: 1. Force a 500 via malformed request | 2. Force a 404 | 3. Inspect bodies | 4. Check for stack traces, SQL, file paths, framework versions
Expected_Result: Generic error pages returned; no stack trace, SQL, internal path or version disclosed; correlation id only

Title: CORS policy is restrictive and does not reflect arbitrary origins
Preconditions: REST/FHIR API reachable cross-origin
Test_Steps: 1. Send request with Origin: https://evil.example | 2. Inspect Access-Control-Allow-Origin and -Credentials | 3. Try null origin | 4. Try preflight
Expected_Result: Arbitrary origins not reflected; ACAO restricted to allowlist; credentials not allowed with wildcard; null origin denied

Title: Unsafe HTTP methods (TRACE/PUT/OPTIONS) restricted
Preconditions: Web/app server reachable
Test_Steps: 1. Send TRACE request | 2. Send arbitrary PUT to static path | 3. Inspect Allow header on OPTIONS | 4. Observe
Test_Data: Methods: TRACE, PUT, DELETE on static resources
Expected_Result: TRACE disabled; no unauthorized PUT/DELETE on static; Allow advertises only required methods

Title: Sensitive endpoints (actuator/console/swagger) not publicly exposed
Preconditions: Knowledge of common management paths
Test_Steps: 1. Probe /actuator, /h2-console, /env, /swagger, /api-docs, module debug pages | 2. Observe auth requirement | 3. Check data exposure
Expected_Result: Management/debug consoles disabled or auth-protected in production; no config/secrets exposed anonymously

Title: Account lockout / throttling after repeated failed logins
Preconditions: A valid account exists; lockout policy configured
Test_Steps: 1. Submit N-1 wrong passwords | 2. Submit Nth wrong password to trip lockout | 3. Attempt correct password while locked | 4. Wait/observe unlock or admin reset
Test_Data: threshold=5 attempts; lockout window per policy
Expected_Result: States: Active -> (fails) -> Locked -> (timeout/admin) -> Active; correct password rejected while Locked; events audited

Title: Session identifier rotates on authentication
Preconditions: Login flow issues a session cookie
Test_Steps: 1. Obtain pre-auth session id | 2. Authenticate successfully | 3. Compare post-auth session id | 4. Replay pre-auth id
Expected_Result: Session id is regenerated on login; pre-auth id invalidated; fixation prevented

Title: Session cookies set with HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite
Preconditions: HTTPS session established
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate | 2. Inspect Set-Cookie for session cookie | 3. Verify HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite | 4. Verify scope/path
Expected_Result: Session cookie marked HttpOnly, Secure and SameSite=Lax/Strict; not script-accessible; not sent cross-site insecurely

Title: Logout invalidates session server-side (no token reuse)
Preconditions: Authenticated session active
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate and capture session/token | 2. Logout | 3. Replay the previous session/token on a protected request | 4. Observe
Expected_Result: Post-logout the old session/token is rejected (401/redirect); server-side invalidation, not just client cookie clear

Title: Idle and absolute session timeout enforced
Preconditions: Configured idle timeout (e.g., 30 min) and absolute lifetime
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate | 2. Idle for timeout-1 then act (still valid) | 3. Idle for timeout+1 then act (expired) | 4. Exceed absolute lifetime
Expected_Result: At timeout-1 session valid; at timeout+1 session expired requiring re-auth; absolute lifetime enforced regardless of activity

Title: MFA challenge required and cannot be bypassed
Preconditions: MFA enabled for the account (owned environment)
Test_Steps: 1. Enter valid username/password | 2. At MFA step attempt to skip by calling post-auth endpoint directly | 3. Submit wrong OTP repeatedly | 4. Submit replayed OTP
Test_Data: OTP=valid then replay; skip via direct URL
Expected_Result: MFA cannot be bypassed by direct navigation; wrong OTP throttled; OTP single-use and time-bound

Title: Password complexity, history and length boundaries enforced
Preconditions: Password change available; policy configured
Test_Steps: 1. Set password at min length-1 (reject) | 2. At min length (accept) | 3. Reuse a previous password (reject) | 4. Common/breached password (reject)
Expected_Result: Below-min rejected; at-min accepted; reused-within-history rejected; common/breached rejected; messages non-enumerating

Title: JWT rejects none algorithm and signature stripping
Preconditions: API accepts a bearer JWT (SMART/OAuth on owned env)
Test_Steps: 1. Capture a valid JWT | 2. Re-sign with alg=none and remove signature | 3. Send to protected API | 4. Observe
Expected_Result: Token rejected (401); server enforces expected algorithm; none/unsigned tokens never accepted

Title: JWT signature validation detects payload tampering
Preconditions: Valid signed JWT available
Test_Steps: 1. Decode JWT and modify sub/scope/exp claims | 2. Re-encode without valid signature | 3. Send to API | 4. Observe
Test_Data: modify scope to system/*.*; modify exp to future
Expected_Result: Tampered token rejected; signature mismatch detected; no elevated scope honored

Title: Expired and not-yet-valid JWT are rejected
Preconditions: Ability to mint tokens with controlled exp/nbf on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Send token with exp in the past | 2. Send token with nbf in the future | 3. Send token at exact exp boundary | 4. Observe
Expected_Result: Expired and not-yet-valid tokens rejected; boundary handled consistently; clock skew tolerance documented

Title: OAuth/SMART scope enforcement limits resource and action
Preconditions: Tokens issued with varying scopes on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. With patient/Observation.read attempt Observation read (allow) and write (deny) | 2. With user/MedicationRequest.write attempt write (allow) and Patient.read (deny) | 3. Record matrix
Expected_Result: Each request honored strictly per granted scope; resource and action enforced; out-of-scope denied 403

Title: OAuth redirect_uri strictly validated against allowlist
Preconditions: OAuth/SMART authorization endpoint on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Start authorize with registered redirect_uri (allow) | 2. Use unregistered redirect_uri (deny) | 3. Use open-redirect/path-append variants | 4. Observe
Expected_Result: Only exact-registered redirect_uri accepted; variants and unregistered denied; no token/code leak to attacker URL

Title: Authorization code requires PKCE and code is single-use
Preconditions: Public SMART client using auth code + PKCE
Test_Steps: 1. Complete authorize obtaining code | 2. Exchange code with wrong code_verifier (deny) | 3. Exchange with correct verifier (allow) | 4. Replay the code a second time (deny)
Expected_Result: Wrong verifier rejected; correct verifier succeeds once; code replay rejected; PKCE required for public clients

Title: Audit/log records cannot be altered or deleted by users
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; a clinical action recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Perform an auditable action | 2. Attempt to update the audit record via API/UI | 3. Attempt to delete it | 4. Verify
Expected_Result: Audit entries are append-only; update/delete denied even for admins via app; tamper attempts themselves logged

Title: Software/module updates verified for integrity before load
Preconditions: Module/plugin install mechanism on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to install an unsigned/modified module | 2. Attempt with tampered checksum | 3. Observe load behavior
Expected_Result: Unsigned/tampered modules rejected; integrity (signature/checksum) verified prior to activation

Title: Insecure deserialization of untrusted input rejected
Preconditions: Endpoint accepting serialized/object payloads
Test_Steps: 1. Submit crafted serialized payload | 2. Submit unexpected type/gadget chain marker | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Untrusted deserialization blocked; only expected DTO shapes parsed; no remote code execution; safe 400

Title: Concurrent edit integrity uses version/ETag (lost update prevention)
Preconditions: Two clients editing the same patient/obs
Test_Steps: 1. Client A GET resource with ETag/version | 2. Client B updates the resource | 3. Client A updates using stale ETag (If-Match) | 4. Observe
Expected_Result: Stale update rejected (412 Precondition Failed / conflict); no silent lost update; integrity preserved

Title: Authentication successes and failures are logged with context
Preconditions: Audit/log subsystem enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Perform successful login | 2. Perform failed login | 3. Perform privilege-denied action | 4. Inspect logs
Expected_Result: Each event logged with timestamp, user, source IP, action and outcome; no password/PHI in log payload

Title: PHI read access generates an access/disclosure audit entry
Preconditions: Audit logging of record access enabled (HIPAA)
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient chart as clinician | 2. View an encounter and lab result | 3. Inspect audit trail
Expected_Result: Each PHI access is recorded with who/what/when/patient; supports HIPAA accounting of disclosures; no gaps

Title: Logs do not contain passwords, tokens or full PHI
Preconditions: Access to application/server logs on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger login, token exchange, patient search | 2. Inspect logs for secrets | 3. Search for password, bearer, SSN patterns
Expected_Result: No credentials, tokens or unmasked PHI in logs; sensitive values masked/omitted

Title: Monitoring alerts on brute force and anomalous access
Preconditions: Monitoring/alerting integrated on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Simulate burst of failed logins | 2. Simulate bulk PHI export | 3. Verify alert generation/threshold
Expected_Result: Alerts raised for brute force and bulk-access anomalies within defined thresholds; routed to security responders

Title: Log tampering/gaps are detectable (integrity controls)
Preconditions: Centralized/append-only logging with integrity controls
Test_Steps: 1. Review log integrity mechanism (hash chain/WORM) | 2. Simulate deletion of a log line in copy | 3. Verify detection
Expected_Result: Tampering or gaps detectable via integrity controls; logs forwarded off-host; chain-of-custody preserved

Title: SSRF via external/contained reference resolution blocked
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint that may resolve references/$validate against profiles
Test_Steps: 1. POST resource with reference URL to internal metadata (169.254.169.254) | 2. POST profile/canonical URL to internal host | 3. Observe outbound fetch
Expected_Result: Server does not fetch internal/cloud-metadata URLs; outbound resolution disabled or allowlisted; SSRF blocked

Title: SSRF via document import / webhook URL field
Preconditions: Feature that fetches a user-supplied URL (import, image, webhook)
Test_Steps: 1. Supply URL pointing to internal IP ranges | 2. Supply file:// and gopher:// schemes | 3. Supply DNS-rebinding host | 4. Observe
Expected_Result: Internal IPs, non-http schemes and rebinding blocked; allowlist + scheme/host validation enforced; no internal access

Title: SSRF protection survives HTTP redirect to internal host
Preconditions: URL-fetching feature that follows redirects
Test_Steps: 1. Supply external URL that 302-redirects to 127.0.0.1 | 2. Observe whether redirect target is re-validated | 3. Confirm block
Expected_Result: Redirect targets are re-validated against allowlist; internal redirect target blocked; no follow to internal host

Title: State-changing POST requires anti-CSRF token
Preconditions: Authenticated session in a cookie-based UI flow
Test_Steps: 1. Capture a state-changing request (e.g., void patient) | 2. Replay without CSRF token | 3. Replay with wrong token | 4. Replay with valid token
Expected_Result: Requests without/with invalid CSRF token rejected; only valid same-session token accepted; SameSite as defense in depth

Title: CSRF token is per-session/per-request and not predictable
Preconditions: Authenticated UI session
Test_Steps: 1. Obtain CSRF tokens across multiple requests/sessions | 2. Assess entropy and reuse | 3. Attempt token from session A in session B
Expected_Result: Tokens are unpredictable and bound to the session; cross-session token rejected; not reusable after logout

Title: Framing protection prevents UI redress on clinical actions
Preconditions: App pages reachable; CSP/frame headers configured
Test_Steps: 1. Embed a clinical action page in an iframe on attacker page | 2. Observe frame-ancestors/X-Frame-Options enforcement | 3. Attempt overlay click
Expected_Result: Sensitive pages refuse framing via frame-ancestors 'self'/DENY; clickjacking prevented

Title: API rate limiting on authentication endpoint
Preconditions: Auth/token endpoint reachable on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Send requests just below limit (allowed) | 2. Exceed the limit (throttled) | 3. Inspect 429 and Retry-After | 4. Confirm reset after window
Expected_Result: Below limit allowed; above limit returns 429 with Retry-After; counter resets after window; per-account+IP scoping

Title: Rate limiting on patient search / bulk read prevents scraping
Preconditions: Authenticated low-priv user; search/export endpoints
Test_Steps: 1. Issue rapid sequential searches | 2. Attempt large-page bulk reads | 3. Observe throttling and pagination caps
Expected_Result: Excessive requests throttled; max page size capped; mass-scrape mitigated; events logged

Title: Throttling behavior across auth-state x endpoint-class pairs
Preconditions: Endpoints classed {auth, read, write, export}; auth-states {anon, user, admin}
Test_Steps: 1. For selected pairwise combinations issue load above threshold | 2. Record throttle response | 3. Compare to policy
Expected_Result: Each tested pair throttled per its policy; no pair is unbounded; responses consistent (429 + Retry-After)

Sub_Module: A01 Broken Access Control - Patient list
Title: Patient list/search does not return restricted/embargoed patients
Preconditions: A restricted/VIP patient flagged; clinician without break-glass
Test_Steps: 1. Search for the restricted patient by name | 2. Attempt direct open | 3. Verify break-glass requirement
Expected_Result: Restricted patient hidden from normal results; access requires break-glass with justification; access audited

Title: Break-glass access requires justification and heightened audit
Preconditions: Break-glass workflow enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger break-glass on a restricted chart | 2. Attempt without justification (deny) | 3. Provide justification (allow, time-boxed) | 4. Verify expiry and audit
Expected_Result: Without justification denied; with justification access granted, time-boxed, and logged with elevated detail; auto-expires

Title: Injection payload x input-field pairwise coverage
Preconditions: Authenticated user; multiple input surfaces
Test_Steps: 1. Apply payload classes {SQLi, XSS, LDAPi, CMDi} across fields {name, identifier, note, search} as pairwise set | 2. Submit | 3. Record handling
Test_Data: pairwise matrix of payload x field
Expected_Result: Every tested pair is neutralized (encoded/parameterized/rejected); no execution or data leak in any pair

Title: FHIR API refuses cleartext and enforces TLS
Preconditions: FHIR base URL on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt http:// FHIR call | 2. Inspect redirect/refusal | 3. Confirm https-only capability statement security
Expected_Result: Cleartext FHIR rejected/redirected; CapabilityStatement.security advertises TLS/OAuth; no PHI over http

Title: HL7v2 inbound interface authenticates/authorizes sending app
Preconditions: HL7v2 MLLP listener configured on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A01 from an unregistered sending facility | 2. Send from a registered facility | 3. Observe ACK/NAK
Expected_Result: Unregistered sender rejected (AR/AE NAK); only registered facilities accepted; rejection logged; no patient created from rogue feed

Title: HL7v2 segment/escape injection does not corrupt parsing
Preconditions: HL7v2 inbound processing on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Send message with embedded delimiters/escape sequences in PID fields | 2. Send oversized field | 3. Observe parsing and stored data
Expected_Result: Delimiters/escapes handled safely; no segment injection or field bleed; oversized rejected with NAK; data stored intact

Title: Unauthenticated FHIR access limited to metadata only
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. GET /fhir2/R4/metadata anonymously (allowed) | 2. GET /fhir2/R4/Patient anonymously (deny) | 3. GET /fhir2/R4/Observation anonymously (deny)
Expected_Result: CapabilityStatement may be public; all PHI resources require auth; anonymous resource reads denied 401

Title: Bearer tokens not cached/exposed in browser storage or history
Preconditions: SMART app session in browser
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect localStorage/sessionStorage for raw long-lived tokens | 2. Inspect browser history/URLs for tokens | 3. Check cache headers on token responses
Expected_Result: Tokens not persisted insecurely; token endpoints Cache-Control: no-store; no tokens in URL/history

Sub_Module: A04 Insecure Design - Rate of consent
Title: Consent state gates data sharing/export operations
Preconditions: Patient consent flags configured (share, research, export)
Test_Steps: 1. With consent=denied attempt export/share (deny) | 2. With consent=granted attempt (allow) | 3. Revoke consent then attempt (deny) | 4. Verify audit
Expected_Result: Operations strictly follow current consent state; revocation immediately blocks; each decision audited; fail-closed on unknown

Sub_Module: A01 Broken Access Control - Location scope
Title: User restricted to a location cannot access other location's patients
Preconditions: User assigned to Location L1; patient belongs to Location L2
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as L1 user | 2. Search/open an L2-only patient | 3. Attempt via REST with explicit location filter override
Expected_Result: L2 patient not accessible to L1 user; location-based scoping enforced server-side; override attempt denied and logged

Title: XML/FHIR XML parsing blocks external entity (XXE) injection
Preconditions: Endpoint accepting XML (FHIR XML or HL7 CDA) on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. POST XML with external entity referencing file:///etc/passwd | 2. POST with billion-laughs entity expansion | 3. Observe
Test_Data: XML DOCTYPE with SYSTEM entity; nested entity bomb
Expected_Result: External entities not resolved; no local file disclosure; entity expansion limited; parser hardened (DTD disabled)

Title: API does not over-return fields (excessive data exposure)
Preconditions: Authenticated user with limited need-to-know
Test_Steps: 1. Request a patient resource | 2. Inspect returned fields for unnecessary sensitive data | 3. Compare default vs custom representation
Expected_Result: Default representation limits sensitive fields; no internal-only fields (audit metadata, internal ids beyond uuid) over-exposed; least disclosure

Title: Concurrent session policy and remote session revocation
Preconditions: Policy for concurrent sessions configured; same account on two devices
Test_Steps: 1. Login on device A and device B | 2. Apply policy (limit or list) | 3. Revoke device A session centrally | 4. Act on device A
Expected_Result: Policy enforced (limit or visibility); revoked session immediately invalid; user can terminate remote sessions; events audited

Title: FHIR Provenance/AuditEvent recorded for create/update of clinical data
Preconditions: FHIR write enabled; Provenance/AuditEvent supported on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Create an Observation via FHIR | 2. Update it | 3. Query Provenance/AuditEvent for the resource | 4. Verify actor and target
Expected_Result: Provenance/AuditEvent generated capturing actor, action, timestamp and target version; supports integrity and traceability

Title: Encryption key and secret rotation supported without data loss
Preconditions: Key management/rotation procedure on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Review key rotation procedure for at-rest keys and signing keys | 2. Rotate a signing key (JWKS) | 3. Verify old tokens validate during overlap then expire | 4. Verify data still readable
Expected_Result: Rotation supported with overlap window; old kid honored until expiry then retired; at-rest data re-keyed without loss; documented

Title: Audit timestamps are accurate, synchronized and timezone-consistent
Preconditions: NTP-synchronized hosts; audit enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Perform actions across hosts | 2. Compare audit timestamps and timezone handling | 3. Check for drift | 4. Verify UTC storage
Expected_Result: Timestamps synchronized (NTP), stored in UTC/with offset, consistent across components; no drift undermining audit ordering

Title: Batch/bundle request enforces per-entry authorization
Preconditions: FHIR transaction bundle supported on owned env
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a transaction bundle mixing authorized and unauthorized resource ops | 2. Observe per-entry handling | 3. Verify no partial unauthorized success
Expected_Result: Each bundle entry independently authorized; unauthorized entries fail; no bypass of per-resource checks via batching

Title: Header/cookie hardening pairwise across endpoint x context
Preconditions: Endpoints {UI page, REST, FHIR} x contexts {anon, auth}
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise combination inspect security headers and cookie flags | 2. Record presence/correctness | 3. Compare to baseline
Expected_Result: All tested endpoint/context pairs carry correct security headers and cookie flags; no pair missing hardening
